fields.summary: Fix Kyverno policy violation on ingress-nginx-dsp-bidding-systems admission job

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
## Problem

The ingress-nginx admission webhook job in namespace `op-ingress-nginx-dsp-bidding-systems` is blocked by Kyverno policy `require-requests-limits`:

```
one or more objects failed to apply, reason: admission webhook "validate.kyverno.svc-fail" denied the request:

resource Job/op-ingress-nginx-dsp-bidding-systems/ingress-nginx-dsp-bidding-systems-admission-create was blocked due to the following policies

require-requests-limits:
  autogen-validate-resources: 'validation error: - CPU and Memory resource requests
    are required. - Memory resource limit is required.'
```

## Root Cause

The admission-create Job template in the ingress-nginx Helm chart does not have resource requests/limits configured, which violates the cluster Kyverno policy.

## Fix

Add resource requests and limits to the admission webhook job in the ingress-nginx Helm values for the `dsp-bidding-systems` deployment. The relevant container at `/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources/limits/` needs:

* CPU request
* Memory request
* Memory limit

## Reference

* Policy docs: https://adgear.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/20158808082/Require+requests+limits

fields.summary: AI skill for IaC engineering standards

issuetype.description: A user story. Created by JIRA Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.description:
Establish a foundational skill that encodes organizational Terraform conventions, compliance requirements, and engineering best practices — serving as the guidance layer for all infrastructure provisioning skills.

###   Acceptance Criteria:

* Adapts JPL/NASA Power of 10 rules to Terraform context
* Adapts Terraform best practices (<custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">http://terraform-best-practices.com</custom> )
* Adapts custom Samsung Ads rules (allowed providers/modules list from TFE)
* Follows AWS resource naming conventions (references aws-naming skill)
* Defines naming convention rules for Terraform resources (locals, variables, outputs, modules)
* Skill is tested and validated in a real provisioning scenario

fields.summary: Streamline the provisioning of Claude code review action to a new repo

issuetype.description: A user story. Created by JIRA Software - do not edit or delete.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.description:
We’ve enabled Claude code review for a few repos in a bit of a hacky manner - I’ve downloaded the private key for the app locally, and when someone asks for a new repo to be added, I upload the secrets to that new repo as repo secrets. Let’s streamline this - the secrets should be in Vault, and there should be instructions in Confluence describing how to enable Claude Code review in a new repo.  
  
**Additional Info:**  
**Requirements:**  
      **PR reviews using the** `claude code`  
**Present Scenario and Analysis:**  
     Currently the PR reviews using the `claude code` is being setup up at a individual repo level with the below steps

* Setup the secrets at the Github Level for individual repo
* Individual IAM role is being created for each repo in `adgeardev` Manually
* Github Workflow is being setup at the individual repo

**Solution and Next Steps:**  
    I am working on streamlining the PR review process using the `claude code` at an organization level using the  `REVIEW.md` rules file with the below steps

* Setup the secrets in `Vault` accessible to all repo(s).
* Setup a common IAM role using Terraform
* **TBD:** centralized workflow(s) with organization level code review rules and custom rules for the repo(s).

fields.description:
The metadata check already is configured to run on all repos, but it’s not mandatory.

We previously made it mandatory and ran into an obstacle - auto-generated PRs (like what happens on kserve) don’t trigger the metadata check.

A part of the success criteria for this task is that we don’t block these auto-generated PRs
